classification.question_text: A light, vertical, copper wire of length 2.4 m and uniform cross-sectional area 2.0 × 10m supports a load of mass 4.0 kg. 11 The Young modulus of copper is 1.2 × 10Pa. What is the strain energy in the wire? –1 (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg; assume that the wire obeys Hooke’s law and that the cross-sectional area remains constant) –5 A 8.0 × 10J – 4 B 1.7 × 10J – 4 C 4.0 × 10J –3 D 8.0 × 10J –2 E 4.0 × 10J –2 F 1.6 × 10J

questions.text: The three internal angles in a triangle are α,β andθ , and 3 tan α – 2 sinβ = 2 5 tan α + 6 sinβ = 8 What is the value ofθ in degrees? A 15 B 45 C 75 D 105 E 135

questions.text: Light travelling in a transparent liquid strikes the surface from below. The angle between the surface of the liquid and the direction of travel of the light is α. The light then travels along the surface between the liquid and the air as shown in the diagram. α Now, light travelling in air strikes the surface from above so that the angle between the surface and the direction of travel of this light is also α. After the light strikes the surface from above, the angle between the surface and the direction of travel of the refracted light is β. Which expression gives β ? (all angles are in degrees) 2 A cos β = cosα –1 ⎛1⎞ B cos β = cos α sin⎜⎟ ⎝α⎠ 2 C sin β = sinα 2 D β = 90 – (90 – α) E β = 0 F β = α

classification.question_text: Light travelling in a transparent liquid strikes the surface from below. The angle between the surface of the liquid and the direction of travel of the light is α. The light then travels along the surface between the liquid and the air as shown in the diagram. α Now, light travelling in air strikes the surface from above so that the angle between the surface and the direction of travel of this light is also α. After the light strikes the surface from above, the angle between the surface and the direction of travel of the refracted light is β. Which expression gives β ? (all angles are in degrees) 2 A cos β = cosα –1 ⎛1⎞ B cos β = cos α sin⎜⎟ ⎝α⎠ 2 C sin β = sinα 2 D β = 90 – (90 – α) E β = 0 F β = α

questions.text: The velocity–time graph is for an 80 kg person in a lift that is moving vertically upwards. velocity / m s–1 2.0 X 0.0 0.05.010.0 20.0 25.0 time / s What is the magnitude of the contact force between the person and the lift floor at the time corresponding to X? –1 (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg) A 640 N B 768 N C 800 N D 832 N E 960 N END OF TEST 33 34 35
